name: Coquilles St. Jacques
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine chicken stock, white wine, bay leaf, parsley, thyme, peppercorns, and fennel in a saucepan. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer uncovered for 20 minutes.
2. Add the scallops to the pan and cook for an additional 10 minutes. Remove the scallops with tongs and set aside.
3. Strain the stock through a fine-mesh sieve into a bowl, leaving behind solids until reduced to one cup.
4. In a separate saucepan, melt butter over medium heat. Add sliced mushrooms and shallots; sauté until tender. Remove from heat and transfer to the bowl with scallops using tongs.
5. Sprinkle flour over the butter mixture in the pan. Cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
6. Stir in the reduced stock and remaining cream. Bring to a simmer, then add egg yolks mixed with lemon juice and additional cream. Return to heat and cook until just emulsified.
7. Adjust seasoning as needed.
8. Divide the scallop/mushroom mixture evenly among six shallow dishes or ramekins. Spoon half of the sauce over each dish.
9. Sprinkle bread crumbs over the top of each serving. Place under a broiler for 2-3 minutes until lightly browned.
10. Serve immediately.
